title: ficus pumila var. awkeotsang
intro: Ficus pumila var. awkeotsang, also known as the creeping fig, is a variety of the common fig tree that is native to Asia. It is used in traditional Chinese medicine, known as 'Awkeotsang', with medicinal properties attributed to its ability to cool the blood and relieve fever. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially lowering blood pressure. In traditional Chinese medicine, it is often prepared as a decoction or infused in a tea, and may also be used in combination with other herbs in various herbal preparations.
